tier_justification: Single work-unit, no outward/irreversible surface (comment-only addition to a test file header, zero executable-line changes), and an existing test (install_mode_sweep.test.sh itself) already covers the goal state the task investigates — the test's pass/fail behavior is the oracle for whether the nested-worktree condition reproduces, and it is unchanged by this diff. The investigation's evidence (reproduction transcript) is the PR-body artifact called for by the plan's anti-rubber-stamp check, not a new scripted assertion.

evals.assert: install_mode_sweep.test.sh exits 0 with all checks ok when run from inside a worktree checked out under .claude/worktrees/ at final main
evals.cmd: git worktree add .claude/worktrees/wu4-verify origin/main --detach && (cd .claude/worktrees/wu4-verify && bash hooks/scripts/tests/install_mode_sweep.test.sh); echo EXIT=$?; git worktree remove --force .claude/worktrees/wu4-verify
evals.negative_control: Same command run against a commit before scripts/lib/git-common.sh etc. were set to 100644 in the index (or with the sweep's mode-read logic deliberately broken) fails one or more `check` lines and exits 1 — proving the test can fail, not just always pass.

evals.assert: hooks/scripts/tests/run_all.sh still reports all suites passed at final main (no regression introduced by the header comment)
evals.negative_control: Deliberately reintroducing a syntax error in install_mode_sweep.test.sh's edited header region (e.g. an unterminated comment) makes run_all.sh report a failing suite instead of "run_all: N/N suites passed".

evals.assert: The PR that lands this change carries the literal nested-worktree reproduction transcript in its body, not just a header comment (anti-rubber-stamp requirement from the loop plan)
evals.negative_control: A PR body containing only prose ("investigated, did not reproduce") with zero literal `ok   -` / `FAIL -` test-output lines would return 0 and fail this check.
